title: T-050 — Authorization policy foundation contract for org-scoped permissions and preflight helpers
prompt:
# T-050 — Authorization policy foundation contract for org-scoped permissions and preflight helpers

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §3, §5.x, §8, §W0-v4.3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
ACP import shape: top-level TaskCreate only (`title`, `prompt`, `labels`, `priority`, `max_attempts`, `pipeline_name`, `pipeline_inputs`). Do not add generated ACP fields such as task_id/status/project_id. `pipeline_inputs.root_path` is mandatory.

## Goal
Define the foundation authorization policy contract so Settings/NPD/Technical tasks implement one consistent org-scoped permission model.

## Locked Wave 0 decisions to honor
- Business data-plane scope is `org_id`; do not create new business-table `tenant_id` columns. Tenant/control-plane concepts may exist above org scope, but app/business rows, RLS policies, audit/outbox rows, role grants, and R13 skeletons use `org_id`.
- Canonical finished-good domain/event prefix is `fg.*`. `fa.*` / `FA` may appear only as an explicit legacy compatibility alias during migration, never as the primary new contract.
- Use the safe non-spoofable org-context RLS pattern. Do not rely on unsafe direct custom GUC `SET` by app users. Do not declare `LEAKPROOF` unless the implementation proves it is valid and necessary.
- Lower numeric priority is picked sooner; dependencies must be local `T-XXX` IDs. Cross-module blockers, if any, belong in `pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies` and in this prompt.
- Exactly one atomic task type: `T1-schema`.

## Implementation contract
Create `_foundation/contracts/authorization-policy.md`. Define policy layers: identity session, org context resolution, permission enum normalization, role grants, SoD, action preflight, audit. Lock `org_id` as authorization scope, canonical permissions from T-004, legacy aliases normalization only at boundary, no tenant_id business grants. Define helper contract names (`requireOrgPermission`, `preflightAction`, `assertSodGrantAllowed`, `withAuditContext`) but do not implement them. Include Settings Quality placeholder decision: use existing `settings.flags.edit`, not `settings.quality.*`. Include split ownership for future tasks (schema/seed, helpers/actions/preflight, UI) without creating Taskmaster artifacts.

## Files in scope
- _foundation/contracts/authorization-policy.md [create]

## Dependencies
- T-004
- T-047
- T-048

## Cross-module dependencies
- 02-SETTINGS T-122 split consumes this contract

## Acceptance criteria
1. Contract uses `org_id` consistently for authorization scope.
2. Contract references canonical permissions and rejects persisted legacy aliases.
3. Contract includes SoD for org.access.admin/org.schema.admin and dual-control expectation.
4. Contract states Settings Quality placeholder uses `settings.flags.edit` / existing flag model, not `settings.quality.*`.

## Test strategy
- Run grep checks for `org_id`, `settings.flags.edit`, `org.access.admin`, `org.schema.admin`, and absence of `settings.quality.*` as a recommended permission.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement helpers here.
- Do not create new settings.quality permission namespace.
- Do not use tenant_id as business authorization scope.

## Closeout evidence
Provide changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations_from_prd, and git_status.
